title: Upper-Body & Trunk Strength
objective: Keep a real strength dose in the week with meaningful upper-body and trunk work, while fully protecting Friday's sustained-power anchor.
why_now: The week had no strength session, which is a stated gym-identity priority and the weekly minimum-dose rule. Thursday sits the day before Friday's 150-min sustained anchor, so loaded lower-body work is off the table; upper body and trunk carry the dose with no impact on Friday's legs.

targets.notes: 60 minutes. Upper body honest at RPE 6-7 with 1-2 reps in reserve. Trunk controlled. No loaded squat/hinge, no leg fatigue, no finisher - Friday's anchor is the priority.

centr_recommendation.primary: Centr upper-body or push/pull strength session, using the coach table as the prescription
skip: anything that fatigues legs before Friday
centr_recommendation.notes: Use Centr for demos or substitutions; keep everything off the legs.

warmup.notes: Easy cardio plus dynamic upper-body and t-spine mobility.

gym_table.exercise: Dumbbell bench press or machine chest press
Seated dumbbell or machine shoulder press
gym_table.notes: Honest but not grindy; protect the neck/shoulder.
Slow and controlled; no lower-back strain after the weekend digging.

cooldown.notes: Mobility: t-spine, lats, chest, plus gentle hip/back opener after the weekend manual work.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal 2-4h before. If training hungry, 20-30g carbs plus 10-20g protein 30-60 min before.
nutrition.post: Protein-forward meal/snack with 25-35g protein; add carbs since Friday is a big ride.
nutrition.centr_recipe: No new Centr link needed; a normal protein-forward recovery plate is enough.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Protein-forward meal/snack; easy walking if the back/hips feel stiff.
recovery.evening: No extra lower-body work. Fuel and sleep for Friday's anchor.
recovery.next_morning_check: Friday anchor proceeds if legs and back feel normal; if the back is still tender from digging, cap Friday at ~120 min.

skill_focus.action: Keep all work above the waist; brace the trunk without loading the lower back.
skill_focus.why: Strength supports durable position and breathing on long rides, but only if it leaves the legs and back fresh for Friday.

mental_focus.why: The week's key bike stimulus is Friday, so today's job is a real upper-body dose with zero leg cost.
mental_focus.reset: If a set pulls on the lower back, reduce load and shift to fully supported variations.

during_workout_cues: Brace the trunk, spare the lower back.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Add one upper-body accessory set (arms or rear delts), not legs.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Drop the shoulder press or one core block; keep it short.
execution_notes.success_criteria: No leg or lower-back fatigue Friday morning.
